criteria_verification.evidence: Extended FakeNatsClient with async subscribe/unsubscribe and a sync deliver_reply method. Subscribe/unsubscribe events land in the same self.published list used for register/heartbeat so the canonical recording-order assertion remains a single-list inspection. No parallel transport is introduced — the dispatch test composes CorrelationRegistry(transport=nats_client_mock) directly.

criteria_verification.evidence: Verified via `pytest tests/bdd/ -m smoke -q` which collects 5 smoke tests (3 NFI + 2 SAD-011). Each smoke scenario is decorated with @pytest.mark.smoke + @pytest.mark.key_example, mirroring the @smoke / @key-example Gherkin tags. The two smoke scenarios are exact-tool-dispatch and coach-output-parsing.

Each smoke scenario is decorated with @pytest.mark.smoke + @pytest.mark.key_example, mirroring the @smoke / @key-example Gherkin tags. criteria_verification.criterion_text: No flaky scenarios. Use the deterministic `Clock` fixture from
criteria_verification.evidence: assert_subscribe_before_publish reads nats_client_mock.published, builds the list of topic strings, and asserts topics.index("reply.subscribe") < topics.index("dispatch.publish"). Both events land in the same recorder list (subscribe via the extended FakeNatsClient.subscribe; publish via _FakeDispatchPublisher.publish_dispatch), so the comparison is a deterministic recording-order check exactly matching the LES1 invariant.
criteria_verification.notes: assert_subscribe_before_publish reads nats_client_mock.published, builds the list of topic strings, and asserts topics.index("reply.subscribe") < topics.index("dispatch.publish"). Both events land in the same recorder list (subscribe via the extended FakeNatsClient.subscribe; publish via _FakeDispatchPublisher.publish_dispatch), so the comparison is a deterministic recording-order check exactly matching the LES1 invariant.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with
criteria_verification.evidence: TimeoutCoordinator is wired with the conftest's FakeClock via a thin _DispatchClockAdapter (Clock.now passthrough). The specialist-reply round-trip is delivered synchronously by the fake publisher when publish_dispatch is recorded — no time.sleep/asyncio.sleep anywhere. The hard-cut-off path is bounded by the TimeoutCoordinator's own asyncio.timeout (5 s sentinel) but is never reached in the happy paths because the reply lands in the same loop tick.

- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
